headline: Microsoft’s 3,200 Acres Data Center Land Purchase—and the Utility Stock That Benefits
summary: Black Hills Corp.  shares rose on Tuesday after  Microsoft  announced plans to purchase approximately 3,200 acres in Cheyenne, Wyo., to develop a data center.  The project will add to Microsoft’s footprint in the state, as hyperscalers such as  Meta Platforms  have started to build out data centers in Wyoming.  Microsoft already has 11 data centers in the state, with three more currently being built.

content.title: Microsoft’s 3,200 Acres Data Center Land Purchase—and the Utility Stock That Benefits
content.summary: Black Hills Corp.  shares rose on Tuesday after  Microsoft  announced plans to purchase approximately 3,200 acres in Cheyenne, Wyo., to develop a data center.  The project will add to Microsoft’s footprint in the state, as hyperscalers such as  Meta Platforms  have started to build out data centers in Wyoming.  Microsoft already has 11 data centers in the state, with three more currently being built.

headline: PlanHub Appoints David Fleischman as Chief Product Officer
summary: PlanHub, the leading preconstruction bidding platform and marketplace connecting general contractors, subcontractors, and suppliers across the commercial construction industry, today announced the appointment of David Fleischman as Chief Product Officer (CPO). In his role, Fleischman will lead PlanHub's product strategy and roadmap, with a sharp focus on delivering best-in-class AI-powered products that put customers at the center of every decision.

content.title: PlanHub Appoints David Fleischman as Chief Product Officer
content.summary: PlanHub, the leading preconstruction bidding platform and marketplace connecting general contractors, subcontractors, and suppliers across the commercial construction industry, today announced the appointment of David Fleischman as Chief Product Officer (CPO). In his role, Fleischman will lead PlanHub's product strategy and roadmap, with a sharp focus on delivering best-in-class AI-powered products that put customers at the center of every decision.

thumbnail.caption: Planhub, the leader in commercial construction for its bid management solutions.

headline: FPT Advances Chugoku Electric Power Group’s Digital Transformation Through Microsoft 365 Deployment
summary: HANOI, Vietnam, April 15, 2026--FPT has signed a landmark agreement to provide Microsoft 365 (M365) licenses to Chugoku Electric Power Group - one of Japan’s ten largest regional power utilities, ranked sixth by electricity sales. This milestone underscores FPT’s leadership in Japan’s technology sector and its pivotal role in driving innovation and advancing the digital transformation of the Hiroshima-headquartered group.

content.title: FPT Advances Chugoku Electric Power Group’s Digital Transformation Through Microsoft 365 Deployment
content.summary: HANOI, Vietnam, April 15, 2026--FPT has signed a landmark agreement to provide Microsoft 365 (M365) licenses to Chugoku Electric Power Group - one of Japan’s ten largest regional power utilities, ranked sixth by electricity sales. This milestone underscores FPT’s leadership in Japan’s technology sector and its pivotal role in driving innovation and advancing the digital transformation of the Hiroshima-headquartered group.

headline: Glasswall Brings Defense-Level File Sanitization to Every Government Agency and Business Using Microsoft Outlook
summary: Glasswall, an industry-leading provider of intelligent Zero Trust file protection solutions in both the business-to-government (B2G) and business-to-business (B2B) sectors, has today announced the launch of Glasswall's M365 Outlook Integration, a new capability within its Content Disarm and Reconstruction (CDR) platform designed to protect organizations from malicious email attachments.

headline: DTCC Advances Cloud First Strategy to Modernize Core Market and Digital Market Infrastructures
summary: NEW YORK & LONDON & HONG KONG & SINGAPORE & SYDNEY, April 15, 2026--The Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation (DTCC), the premier post-trade market infrastructure for the global financial services industry, today announced new developments in its Cloud First strategy, including partnerships with public cloud providers, AWS and Microsoft, to advance this multi-year initiative to strengthen the resiliency, scalability, security and speed of DTCC’s core and digital assets platforms.

content.title: DTCC Advances Cloud First Strategy to Modernize Core Market and Digital Market Infrastructures
content.summary: NEW YORK & LONDON & HONG KONG & SINGAPORE & SYDNEY, April 15, 2026--The Depository Trust & Clearing Corporation (DTCC), the premier post-trade market infrastructure for the global financial services industry, today announced new developments in its Cloud First strategy, including partnerships with public cloud providers, AWS and Microsoft, to advance this multi-year initiative to strengthen the resiliency, scalability, security and speed of DTCC’s core and digital assets platforms.

headline: Microsoft Tightens AI Grip With Norway GPUs And Wyoming Data Center Land
summary: Microsoft is expanding its AI infrastructure in Europe and the US, securing over 30,000 Nvidia GPU slots in Norway that were previously expected to support OpenAI's Stargate project. The company is also acquiring land in Wyoming to build additional data center capacity for cloud and AI services. These moves increase Microsoft's control over high value AI compute resources and could influence how large enterprises choose cloud providers. Microsoft (NasdaqGS: MSFT) is leaning further into AI...

headline: S&P 500 Hits Record High on Hopes for Iran Deal: Markets Wrap
summary: (Bloomberg) -- The S&P 500 continued its rally after rising to an all-time high on Wednesday, as a potential peace deal in the Middle East and a strong start to corporate earnings season spurred investor optimism. The benchmark index was up 0.7%, putting it above a late-January record. Bank of America Corp. and Morgan Stanley rose as their equity traders posted strong revenue beats. The tech-heavy Nasdaq 100 was up 1%.The US and Iran are considering extending their ceasefire that ends on Tuesday

headline: Marvell Snaps Its Winning Streak. Why It Can Keep Climbing After 150% Surge.
summary: After an impressive run up in the first part of the month, shares of chip and network technology firm Marvell broke their winning streak Wednesday, falling more than 1% by mid-afternoon on Wednesday.  What’s more, booming data-center demand and custom chip partnerships with Big Tech hyperscalers, should help drive longer-term gains, according to Oppenheimer analyst Rick Schafer.  Before today, shares had risen for five straight sessions for their longest winning streak in more than a year.

content.title: Marvell Snaps Its Winning Streak. Why It Can Keep Climbing After 150% Surge.
content.summary: After an impressive run up in the first part of the month, shares of chip and network technology firm Marvell broke their winning streak Wednesday, falling more than 1% by mid-afternoon on Wednesday.  What’s more, booming data-center demand and custom chip partnerships with Big Tech hyperscalers, should help drive longer-term gains, according to Oppenheimer analyst Rick Schafer.  Before today, shares had risen for five straight sessions for their longest winning streak in more than a year.

headline: OpenAI Says Microsoft 'Limited' Its Reach, Slams Anthropic For 'Inflated $30B Revenue
summary: OpenAI‘s new revenue chief just fired shots at both its biggest partner and its biggest rival in the same memo. Denise Dresser told staff Sunday that the Microsoft Corp partnership has “limited our ability to meet enterprises where they are,” while calling demand through Amazon.com Inc‘s Bedrock platform “frankly staggering” since the two companies announced their deal in late February, according to a memo viewed by CNBC. OpenAI Takes Aim At Anthropic’s Numbers Dresser claimed Anthropic’s report

headline: Stocks Spring Forward in Record-Breaking Day
summary: Buoyed by big gains from the likes of Microsoft and Tesla, the S&P 500 rose 0.8% to 7022.95–rallying into the close to finish comfortably above its previous all-time high set in January.  Technology stocks were the big outperformers.

content.title: Stocks Spring Forward in Record-Breaking Day

headline: Tech Stocks Power S&P 500 and Nasdaq to Records
summary: Surging tech stocks carried the S&P 500 and Nasdaq composite to new records on Wednesday, a milestone in major indexes’ rebound from war-fueled losses.  Buoyed by big gains from the likes of  Microsoft  and  Tesla  the S&P 500 rose 0.8% to 7022.95—rallying into the close to finish comfortably above its previous all-time high set in January.  More recently, though, investors have grown increasingly confident that the U.S. and Iran will eventually reach a peace deal.

content.title: Tech Stocks Power S&P 500 and Nasdaq to Records
content.summary: Surging tech stocks carried the S&P 500 and Nasdaq composite to new records on Wednesday, a milestone in major indexes’ rebound from war-fueled losses.  Buoyed by big gains from the likes of  Microsoft  and  Tesla  the S&P 500 rose 0.8% to 7022.95—rallying into the close to finish comfortably above its previous all-time high set in January.  More recently, though, investors have grown increasingly confident that the U.S. and Iran will eventually reach a peace deal.

headline: Marvell Eeks Out Another Record Close. Why It Can Keep Climbing After 150% Surge.
summary: Shares of chip and network technology firm Marvell closed higher Wednesday after dipping more than 1% earlier in the day to notch their sixth consecutive day of gains and a new record close.  Booming data-center demand and custom chip partnerships with Big Tech hyperscalers, should help drive longer-term gains as well, according to Oppenheimer analyst Rick Schafer.  Schafer said Marvell is well-positioned because of its “full suite” of copper and optical products.

Schafer said Marvell is well-positioned because of its “full suite” of copper and optical products.

headline: AI's Cash Splash Continues: Meta Platforms and Broadcom Expand Custom Chip Partnership
summary: Hyperscalers are ramping AI infrastructure spending to record levels this year, with a string of major partnerships and funding rounds across the sector. Meta’s newly expanded collaboration with Broadcom on custom MTIA chips adds another layer to that build-out, focusing on multi-generation silicon and multi-gigawatt capacity through 2029.

headline: AI trade could be overdue for a rebound amid major stock rally
summary: The major market indexes (^DJI, ^IXIC, ^GSPC) remain in striking distance to new record highs after recent rallies have erased the losses seen since the start of the US-Iran war. Mahoney Asset Management CEO Ken Mahoney and Yahoo Finance Senior Markets Reporter Ines Ferré further examine the AI outlook on tech giants like Microsoft (MSFT), Apple (AAPL), and ASML (ASML).
